description:
You are the storage administrator on this machine.  
  A backup robot will start contacting this host over SSH, so you must create
  a brand-new key pair for the account that you are currently logged in as.
  The test-suite will look for the following final state – please follow the
  details literally.

  1. Create the directory “/home/user/.ssh” if it does not already exist and
     make sure its permissions are exactly 700 (drwx------).

  2. Generate a brand-new Ed25519 key pair **with no passphrase** and the
     comment “backup@storage”.  
     • Store the private key at  
       “/home/user/.ssh/backup_ed25519”  
     • Store the public  key at  
       “/home/user/.ssh/backup_ed25519.pub”  
     • The private key file must have mode 600 (-rw-------).  
       The public key file must have mode 644 (-rw-r--r--).

  3. Append a copy of the complete one-line public key
     (the *entire* contents of “backup_ed25519.pub”)
     to the file “/home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys”.  
     • If “authorized_keys” does not exist, create it.  
     • Its permissions must be 600 (-rw-------).  
     • After you finish, “authorized_keys” must contain **exactly one**
       occurrence of that public-key line.

  4. Produce a very small log file called “/home/user/ssh_setup.log”.  
     The file must contain **exactly three lines** (no leading/trailing
     blanks, no extra blank line at the end) in this precise order:

        PRIVATE_KEY_CREATED
        PUBLIC_KEY_CREATED
        AUTHORIZED_KEYS_UPDATED

     All capitals, underscore-separated, *no* additional text.

  The automated grader will check:
  • The existence and permissions of “/home/user/.ssh”.  
  • That “/home/user/.ssh/backup_ed25519” and
    “/home/user/.ssh/backup_ed25519.pub” exist, are non-empty, and have the
    required permissions.  
  • That the public key line found inside
    “/home/user/.ssh/backup_ed25519.pub” is present exactly once in
    “/home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys”.  
  • That “/home/user/ssh_setup.log” exists and is **exactly** the three
    lines shown above.

  You do not need to communicate with any remote host; everything is local.
  Typical command count is 3-4 shell commands.  When you are done, simply
  exit the shell – no additional output is required.
truth:
The tests will verify the following:

  1. Directory /home/user/.ssh exists with mode 0700 (octal).
  2. File /home/user/.ssh/backup_ed25519 exists:
     • size > 0
     • mode 0600
  3. File /home/user/.ssh/backup_ed25519.pub exists:
     • size > 0
     • mode 0644
  4. File /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys exists:
     • mode 0600
     • contains exactly one line that matches
       the entire contents of /home/user/.ssh/backup_ed25519.pub.
  5. File /home/user/ssh_setup.log exists:
     • SHA-256 of its contents will match the hash of the three-line string
       “PRIVATE_KEY_CREATED\nPUBLIC_KEY_CREATED\nAUTHORIZED_KEYS_UPDATED\n”
       (including the single trailing newline at the end of the third line).
name: task_000000_3a4762bc
